text.title: Growth Social Media Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Growth Social Media Strategy application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Strategy MUST commit to ≤2 primary platforms; 'be everywhere' postures are rejected.
rule.rationale: Solo capacity below 10 hours/week cannot sustain compounding on more than two platforms.

rule.statement: One weekly long-form seed MUST produce ≥3 platform-native cuts; copy-paste cross-posting is rejected.
rule.rationale: Native cuts get 3-5x the reach of copy-pasted versions; the atomization loop is the unit economics of solo content.

rule.statement: Primary platforms MUST be picked by ICP-fit score + audience density, not by founder preference.
rule.rationale: Founder preference produces churn-prone strategies; ICP-fit produces compounding traction.

rule.statement: Each platform MUST carry an explicit engagement quota (replies/day); pure broadcast is rejected.
rule.rationale: Broadcast accounts plateau within 90 days; engagement-heavy accounts compound.

rule.statement: Strategy MUST review at 12 weeks; longer commitments without review trap the operator on the wrong platform.
rule.rationale: 12 weeks is enough data to detect compounding; 24 weeks risks sunk-cost lock-in.
